parameters.name: showUserAccounts
parameters.description:
Only valid for org/platform owners. By default all users/owners will only
see their own user group (auto-created personal group).
NOTE: "user account" refers to a personal group automatically created for each user.
This flag controls whether to show these personal groups or only collaborative groups.

parameters.name: relationship
parameters.description:
- ACCOUNT_MEMBER: ACCOUNT_MEMBER indicates a user is a member of the group (deprecated, prefer GROUP_MEMBER)
 - GROUP_MEMBER: GROUP_MEMBER indicates a user is a member of the group
 - ACCOUNT_OWNER: ACCOUNT_OWNER indicates a user owns/administers the group (deprecated, prefer GROUP_OWNER)
 - GROUP_OWNER: GROUP_OWNER indicates a user owns/administers the group

parameters.name: relationship
parameters.description:
- ACCOUNT_MEMBER: ACCOUNT_MEMBER indicates a user is a member of the group (deprecated, prefer GROUP_MEMBER)
 - GROUP_MEMBER: GROUP_MEMBER indicates a user is a member of the group
 - ACCOUNT_OWNER: ACCOUNT_OWNER indicates a user owns/administers the group (deprecated, prefer GROUP_OWNER)
 - GROUP_OWNER: GROUP_OWNER indicates a user owns/administers the group

parameters.name: statsScope
parameters.description:
- USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ALL: Usage across all accounts/organizations
 - USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ACCOUNT: Usage within the user's account
 - USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ORGANIZATION: Usage within the user's organization

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
- USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ALL: Usage across all accounts/organizations
 - USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ACCOUNT: Usage within the user's account
 - USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ORGANIZATION: Usage within the user's organization

parameters.name: trackView
parameters.description: optional, set to true to track as a "viewed" event for usage stats

patch.summary: TODO: given this is more of a backend support item we may want to remove this from the public api.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: TODO: given this is more of a backend support item we may want to remove this from the public api.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Deprecated as this was not used and we need ability to filter by multiple statuses.

 - STAGE_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: also used for pending

parameters.name: requestedStatuses
parameters.description:
Allows multiple statuses to be requested.

 - STAGE_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: also used for pending

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
- SCOPE_USER: SCOPE_USER returns workflows for the user within the currently selected group
 - SCOPE_ACCOUNT: SCOPE_ACCOUNT returns workflows for the group (deprecated, prefer SCOPE_GROUP)
 - SCOPE_GROUP: SCOPE_GROUP returns workflows for the entire group

get.summary:
Get workflow summary details for group owners
NOTE: "Account owner" refers to group owners (deprecated terminology).

Get workflow users for group owners
NOTE: "Account owner" refers to group owners (deprecated terminology).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the container to execute the command in.

parameters.name: rank
parameters.description: Rank of the container to execute the command in.

get.summary: Get the status on a workflow

patch.summary: ScoreWorkflow returns the score for a workflow definition.

Access.Type.title: - BLOCK: BLOCK is not supported yet

Account.description:
Account represents a group within an organization.
NOTE: The term "Account" is deprecated in favor of "Group". This message
represents a collaborative group of users within an organization, not to be
confused with user authentication credentials. The name remains "Account"
for backwards compatibility with existing API clients.

totalAccountUsers.title: total_account_users is the total number of users in the group

AccountOwnerWorkflowsSummary.description:
AccountOwnerWorkflowsSummary provides workflow statistics for group owners.
NOTE: "Account owner" refers to group owners (deprecated terminology).

AccountOwnerWorkflowsSummaryDetails.description:
AccountOwnerWorkflowsSummaryDetails provides detailed workflow info for group owners.
NOTE: "Account owner" refers to group owners (deprecated terminology).

AccountRelationship.description:
AccountRelationship defines a user's role within a group.
NOTE: The term "Account" is deprecated. This enum represents group membership
relationships. Use GROUP_MEMBER and GROUP_OWNER when these values are added
in a future API version.

 - ACCOUNT_MEMBER: ACCOUNT_MEMBER indicates a user is a member of the group (deprecated, prefer GROUP_MEMBER)
 - GROUP_MEMBER: GROUP_MEMBER indicates a user is a member of the group
 - ACCOUNT_OWNER: ACCOUNT_OWNER indicates a user owns/administers the group (deprecated, prefer GROUP_OWNER)
 - GROUP_OWNER: GROUP_OWNER indicates a user owns/administers the group

ApplicationOwnerKind.title:
- GROUP: default
 - ACCOUNT: deprecated alias for GROUP
 - ORGANIZATION: must be an organization owner to use
 - PROVIDER: only available via direct sql updates (for now)

Completed.description: TODO: Remove from public API as this is just an anchor for DAG execution.

hardwareThreads.title: list of hardware threads of the core (nil for threads)

socketPackage.title: socket package associated to the core

numaNode.title: NUMA node associated to the core

category.title: if not provided a default will be set

Defaults.description:
Default defines the default settings that should be applied to all jobs in the workflow.
These can be overridden at the job level.

env.description:
env is a list of environment variables to be exposed inside all job
containers.  Will be overridden by job level env variables.

mounts.description:
mounts is a map containing a key that references a volume by name and
value containing a specification with details of how that volume should
be mounted into the job container.

policy.description:
policy contains configurable rules that define high level execution
behavior of a job such as max execution time and retries on failure.

resource.description:
resource specifies the hardware requirements that must be met for the job
to be executed. This is used by the scheduler to determine if and where a
job can be scheduled to run.

location.description:
location specifies the destination within the container filesystem to
mount a volume directory. This must be an absolute path.

Defaults.Job.Mount.description:
Mount is the specification for how a volume gets mounted into a job
container.

cpu.description: cpu details CPU resource requirements for the job.

memory.description: memory details memory requirements for the job.

devices.description: device details device resource requirements for the job.

exclusive.description:
exclusive sets the requirement that this job is the only one running on
a node.

annotations.description:
annotations is a map of string key value pairs that can be used to
specify custom behavior implemented by the workflow system to meet
needs beyond what the first class resource parameters currently
provide. Potential use cases could be along the lines of:matching a job
to a specfic node pool, requiring a specific CPU microarchitecture or
GPU family, etc.

Defaults.Job.Resource.description:
Resource specifies hardware requirements that must be met for the
execution of a job.

affinity.description:
affinity dictates how the binding of tasks to the specific hardware
layout occurs. Must be one of "NUMA", "SOCKET", or "CORE". An unset
affinity defaults to "CORE".

cores.description: cores is the number of processor cores required.

threads.description:
threads dictates if hardware threads of allocated cores are exposed
to the job container.

sockets.description: sockets is the number of sockets required.

Defaults.Job.Resource.Cpu.description: CPU contains relevant details about CPU requirements.

size.description:
size is the amount of memory required for the job. If no unit is
specified, byte is the default. E.g. 32KiB, 1GB, 32GiB.

byCore.description: by_core defines if the memory is specified per CPU core.

Defaults.Job.Resource.Memory.description: Memory contains relevant details about RAM requirements.

name.description: name is the name of the job to depend on.

status.title:
status is the status that this dependency must be in before this job can run.
Valid values (case-insensitive): RUNNING (Services Only), FINISHED

description.description: description is a human readable description of the dependency.

Dependency.description: Dependency is a concrete dependency that must be satisfied before this job can run.

numa-node.description: Numa node associated to the device.

allocated.title:
Is the device allocated, only used for fuzzball.substrate.api.substrate.v1.ResourceShowResponse,
this field is ignored when set by a plugin returning DeviceInitResponse

portName.description: The name of the port defined above to proxy to.

protocol.title:
The endpoint's intended protocol.
Valid values are: http, https, grpc, grpcs, tcp, tls

type.title:
The type of endpoint to create.
Valid values are: subdomain, path
subdomain - creates a subdomain endpoint like myservice.workflow_id.account_id.fuzzball
path - creates a path based endpoint like /endpoints/account_id/workflow_id/myservice

scope.title:
The scope defines who can access this endpoint.
Valid values are: user, group, organization, public
user - only the workflow creator can access
group - anyone in the same account can access
organization - anyone in the same organization can access
public - no authentication required
Default: group (if not specified)

ExecAction.title: ExecAction represents a command-based probe (matches Kubernetes exactly)

source.description:
Source is the origin of the data. The type of URIs supported are
determined by whether this is specified under ingress or egress.

destination.description:
Destination is the location to move the data. The type of URIs
supported are determined by whether this is specified under ingress or
egress.

policy.description:
policy contains configurable rules that define high level execution
behavior of a data transfer such as max execution time and retries on
failure.

File.description: File specifies details of workflow volume data movement.

GRPCAction.title: GRPCAction represents a gRPC probe (matches Kubernetes exactly)

GetAccountOwnerWorkflowsSummaryDetailsRe.description:
GetAccountOwnerWorkflowsSummaryDetailsResponse returns workflow details for group owners.
NOTE: "Account owner" refers to group owners (deprecated terminology).

GetAccountOwnerWorkflowsUsersResponse.description:
GetAccountOwnerWorkflowsUsersResponse returns workflow users for group owners.
NOTE: "Account owner" refers to group owners (deprecated terminology).

HTTPGetAction.title: HTTPGetAction represents an HTTP GET probe (matches Kubernetes exactly)

HTTPHeader.title: HTTPHeader represents an HTTP header for HTTP probes (matches Kubernetes exactly)

isolated.description:
isolated forces the use of a container network namespace.
This is required for reaching a job container port with port
forwarding.

exposeTcp.description: expose_tcp is a list of container TCP ports to expose.

exposeUdp.description: expose_udp is a list of container UDP ports to expose.

Job.Network.description:
Network defines if the job should be run inside its own network namespace
and enabled optional exposure of job container ports.
This is required if you want to expose ports from the job container
directly or via port forwarding.

ListWorkflowsScope.title:
- SCOPE_USER: SCOPE_USER returns workflows for the user within the currently selected group
 - SCOPE_ACCOUNT: SCOPE_ACCOUNT returns workflows for the group (deprecated, prefer SCOPE_GROUP)
 - SCOPE_GROUP: SCOPE_GROUP returns workflows for the entire group

Mode.title:
- MODE_UNSPECIFIED: SINGLE_NODE_XXX are not supported yet
 - MULTI_NODE_SINGLE_WRITER: not supported yet

nodes.description: nodes is the number of nodes to run job across in parallel.

implementation.title:
implementation is the specific implementation of multinode to be used.
This must match the implementation used by the application within your
job container because different multinode implementations require
slightly different wire up configurations. Must be one of: "ompi",
"openmpi", "mpich", "gasnet"

procsPerNode.description:
procs_per_node specifies the number of processes to spawn
within each job container. If not specified, this value defaults to
the number of CPUs allocated to the job container. The total number
of processes spawned for a job can be calculated by multiplying
this value by the number of nodes requested.

Multinode.description:
Multinode enables a job to run multiple containers across multiple nodes
in parallel by leveraging various implementation for communication and
synchronization.

NameArg.title:
- ACCOUNT_ID: ACCOUNT_ID for volume naming (deprecated, prefer GROUP_ID)
 - GROUP_ID: GROUP_ID for volume naming

cores.title: list of CPU cores on a system

freeCores.title: number of free CPU cores (hardware threads are not accounted)

memoryTotalBytes.title: total memory available on a system

memoryFreeBytes.title: free memory available on a system

memoryBytesByCore.title: rough estimation of the amount of memory available by CPU core

numaNodes.title: NUMA nodes available on a system

NullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

cores.title: list of CPU cores associated to a NUMA node

freeCores.title: number of free CPU cores (hardware threads are not accounted)

memoryTotalBytes.title: total memory available on a NUMA node

memoryFreeBytes.title: free memory available on a NUMA node

memoryBytesByCore.title: rough estimation of the amount of memory available by CPU core

options.title: list of options for the input

Ownership.title:
- ACCOUNT: ACCOUNT is supported with group ownership (deprecated, prefer GROUP)
 - GROUP: GROUP ownership allows all group members to access the volume

timeout.description: timeout specifies rules related to the duration of a workflow step.

retry.description:
retry specifies rules related to rerunning a workflow step in case of
failure.

Policy.description:
Policy contains configurable rules for the execution of a workflow step.
The default policy for a step will set a value for timeout if a custom one
is not specified.

name.description: The name used to refer to this port.

port.description: The port number exposed by the service container.

protocol.title: The protocol used by this port. Valid values are: tcp, udp

exec.title: Probe action (exactly one must be specified)

Probe.title: Probe defines Kubernetes-compatible health checking configuration

total.description: total is the total available resource on the compute service.

free.description: free is the currently free resource on the compute service.

node.description: node is the detailed node information of the compute service.

annotations.description: annotations is the list of annotations associated with the node.

attempts.description:
attempts is the number of retry attemps before failing the step
completely.

Retry.description:
Retry specifies rules related to rerunning a workflow step in case of
failure.

Scope.title:
- ACCOUNT: ACCOUNT scope limits visibility to group members (deprecated, prefer GROUP)
 - GROUP: GROUP scope limits visibility to group members

clusterId.title: optional cluster ID when submitting jobs from a federate cluster

name.description:
Used internally to store the job name.  If this field is present in a
definition then it will be ignored in preference for the map key used in
the `jobs` field.

image.title:
Image is the URI to the container image to be used for this job.
Supported uri formats are: "docker://..." and "oras://" for OCI
and SIF(Singularity Image Format) images respectively.
E.g. docker://alpine:latest

command.description: command is the arguments executed by the container process.

env.description:
env is a list of environment variables to be exposed inside of the job
container.

mounts.description:
mounts is a map containing a key that references a volume by name and
value containing a specification with details of how that volume should
be mounted into the job container.

cwd.description:
cwd allows the specification of the the working dirctory used by the job.
This must be an absolute path. If unset the working directory defaults
first to the working directory in the OCI image config if present and
then to "/".

requires.description:
requires is a list of prerequisite jobs/services in the workflow that must be
completed before this service may be started. This allows jobs within a
workflow to be formed into a directed acyclic graph. If requirements
specified by different jobs results in a cycle, the workflow will fail to
start.

Deprecated: use the `dependsOn` field instead.
Ignored if `dependsOn` is set.
If used instead of `dependsOn`, dependencies are assumed to be "when jobN has finished".

resource.description:
resource specifies the hardware requirements that must be met for the job
to be executed. This is used by the scheduler to determine if and where a
job can be scheduled to run.

multinode.description: multinode enables a service to run across multiple nodes in parallel.

args.title: args passed to above script field, can also be passed to existing command

dependsOn.description: dependsOn is a list of concrete dependencies that must be satisfied before this service can run.

persist.description:
defines if the service should run even if there are no running or waiting jobs in the workflow.
If persist is set to false, the service is stopped when all dependant jobs and services have stopped.
If persist is set to true, the service runs even if there are no dependant jobs/services and continues until the workflow is cancelled.

readinessProbe.title: readinessProbe controls the STARTED → RUNNING transition of the service (required for proper lifecycle monitoring)

endpoints.description: Configures fuzzball endpoints to expose a service's functionality.

Service.Network.description:
Network defines if the job should be run inside its own network namespace
and enabled optional exposure of job container ports.
This is required if you want to expose ports from the job container
directly or via port forwarding.

cores.title: list of CPU cores associated to a socket

freeCores.title: number of free CPU cores (hardware threads are not accounted)

numaNodes.title: list of NUMA nodes associated to a socket

clusterId.title: optional cluster ID when submitting jobs from a federate cluster

applicationId.title: optional application ID to track application usage stats

TCPSocketAction.title: TCPSocketAction represents a TCP socket probe (matches Kubernetes exactly)

start.description: start is the start of the range for Task ID values. Inclusive.

end.description: end is the end of the Task ID values. Inclusive.

concurrency.description:
concurrency is a limit on the number of tasks that can be run in
parallel. The max concurrency for a workflow job is 200.

TaskArray.description:
TaskArray allows a job to be broken up in an embarrassingly parallel
manner for more effective execution. TaskArray jobs are automatically
populated with a `FB_TASK_ID` environment variable to indicate the index
within the task array range of the currently executing task to the
application.

execute.description:
execute  is the maximum allowed duration of a workflow step to run
before it is failed.

Timeout.description: Timeout specifies rules related to the duration of a workflow step.

uri.description: uri is the uri of the resource to be accessed.

secrets.description:
secrets is a map of key value pairs to use to access resource.
The key specifies the type of data that is being used and the value
specifies raw data or a template for the workflow system to populate with
data internally from the secrets engine.

URI.description:
URI contains the specification for a resource to access as well as
additional data necessary for access such as credentials.

UpdateUserProfileSettingsRequest.description: TODO: given this is more of a backend support item we may want to remove this from the public api.

UsageStatsScope.title:
- USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ALL: Usage across all accounts/organizations
 - USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ACCOUNT: Usage within the user's account
 - USAGE_STATS_SCOPE_ORGANIZATION: Usage within the user's organization

name.description:
Name is the key value of the volume in the volume map containing this
struct. This is normally set internally by client side code when parsing
workflow DSLs, but will need to be set when calling the API directly.

type.title:
type is the kind of volume. This currently determines the lifetime of the
volume and the data contained within it.
Ephemeral volumes are empty upon creation and have a lifespan of the
workflow duration. Upon workflow completion the volume and its data are
deleted. Host volumes represent directories on the shared filesystems of
the nodes themselves. They must be pre-existing and fall under
directories specified in the shared-fs-roots config property of the
Volume service. Must be one of "EPHEMERAL" or "HOST".
Deprecated in favor of reference

ingress.description:
Ingress specifies data that should be fetched and made available within
the volume.

egress.description:
Egress specifies data that exists within the volume that should be
exported to another storage location.

path.title:
Path is specified for host volume types with the absolute path to the
directory to mount.
Deprecated: will be removed

reference.description: Volume reference this volume is referring to.

Volume.description:
Volume is an instance of a data volume used within the scope of a workflow.
There are a couple different types of volumes that have different data
retention behavior. Volumes are the means of managing data for compute
applications to process over the course of a workflow and orchestrate
data movement to bring injest data into the bounds of a workflow for
computation or export data generated from computation it to other storage
locations.

TODO(cedric): deprecate type/path fields
 reserved 2, 5;
 reserved "type", "path";

maxByAccount.title: max_by_account limits volumes per group (deprecated naming, represents group limit)

accountId.title: account_id is the group ID this workflow belongs to (deprecated naming, represents a group)

version.description: version defines the version of the workflow document.

volumes.description:
volumes is a map defining volumes to use for this workflow by name.
Map keys define the name of the volume and values are specifications
detailing the data volume. Volume names are used by different
components of the workflow to reference a particular volume instance.

jobs.description:
jobs is a map defining jobs to run as a part of the workflow.
Map keys define the name of the job and values are specifications
detailing the compute job. Job names are used by different components
of the workflow to reference a particular compute job instance.

completed.description: TODO: Remove from public API as this is just an anchor for DAG execution.

annotations.title:
Annotations for the whole workflow. Currently, this is used by the
scheduler to place jobs in a certain topology zone

files.description:
files is a map defining files that may be injected into volumes and jobs.
Map keys define the name of the file and values are the file's contents.
Files names are used by job and volume.ingress components
to reference a specific file.

WorkflowDefinition.description:
WorkflowDefinition is the highest level unit of work for a Fuzzball cluster.
Workflows consist of orchestrating data volumes, container images and
container execution across a compute cluster.

name.description:
Used internally to store the job name.  If this field is present in a
definition then it will be ignored in preference for the map key used in
the `jobs` field.

image.title:
Image is the URI to the container image to be used for this job.
Supported uri formats are: "docker://..." and "oras://" for OCI
and SIF(Singularity Image Format) images respectively.
E.g. docker://alpine:latest

command.description: command is the arguments executed by the container process.

env.description:
env is a list of environment variables to be exposed inside of the job
container.

mounts.description:
mounts is a map containing a key that references a volume by name and
value containing a specification with details of how that volume should
be mounted into the job container.

cwd.description:
cwd allows the specification of the the working dirctory used by the job.
This must be an absolute path. If unset the working directory defaults
first to the working directory in the OCI image config if present and
then to "/".

requires.description:
requires is a list of prerequisite jobs/services in the workflow that must be
completed before this job may be started. This allows jobs within a
workflow to be formed into a directed acyclic graph. If requirements
specified by different jobs results in a cycle, the workflow will fail to
start.

Deprecated: use the `dependsOn` field instead.
Ignored if `dependsOn` is set.
If used instead of `dependsOn`, dependencies are assumed to be "when jobN has finished".

policy.description:
policy contains configurable rules that define high level execution
behavior of a job such as max execution time and retries on failure.

resource.description:
resource specifies the hardware requirements that must be met for the job
to be executed. This is used by the scheduler to determine if and where a
job can be scheduled to run.

multinode.description: multinode enables a job to run across multiple nodes in parallel.

taskArray.description:
task_array enables a job to be broken up into multiple tasks that run
concurrently. This is useful for doing embarrassingly parallel compute
work.

network.title: network defines if the job should run inside its own netowrk namespace

args.title: args passed to above script field, can also be passed to existing command

dependsOn.description: dependsOn is a list of concrete dependencies that must be satisfied before this job can run.

WorkflowDefinition.Job.description:
Job is a single compute "step" in a workflow.
There are a variety of different types of jobs that
result in one or more containers being spawned.
By default, a single container is spawned unless the
TaskArray or Multinode fields are specified.

location.description:
location specifies the destination within the container filesystem to
mount a volume directory. This must be an absolute path.

WorkflowDefinition.Job.Mount.description:
Mount is the specification for how a volume gets mounted into a job
container.

cpu.description: cpu details CPU resource requirements for the job.

memory.description: memory details memory requirements for the job.

devices.description: device details device resource requirements for the job.

exclusive.description:
exclusive sets the requirement that this job is the only one running on
a node.

annotations.description:
annotations is a map of string key value pairs that can be used to
specify custom behavior implemented by the workflow system to meet
needs beyond what the first class resource parameters currently
provide. Potential use cases could be along the lines of:matching a job
to a specfic node pool, requiring a specific CPU microarchitecture or
GPU family, etc.

WorkflowDefinition.Job.Resource.description:
Resource specifies hardware requirements that must be met for the
execution of a job.

affinity.description:
affinity dictates how the binding of tasks to the specific hardware
layout occurs. Must be one of "NUMA", "SOCKET", or "CORE". An unset
affinity defaults to "CORE".

cores.description: cores is the number of processor cores required.

threads.description:
threads dictates if hardware threads of allocated cores are exposed
to the job container.

sockets.description: sockets is the number of sockets required.

WorkflowDefinition.Job.Resource.Cpu.description: CPU contains relevant details about CPU requirements.

size.description:
size is the amount of memory required for the job. If no unit is
specified, byte is the default. E.g. 32KiB, 1GB, 32GiB.

byCore.description: by_core defines if the memory is specified per CPU core.

WorkflowDefinition.Job.Resource.Memory.description: Memory contains relevant details about RAM requirements.

name.description: Name of the container to execute the command in.

rank.description: Rank of the container to execute the command in.

name.description: Name of the container to execute the command in.

rank.description: Rank of the container to execute the command in.
